flashcards.front: What differentiates Gram-positive from Gram-negative bacteria in staining?
flashcards.back: Gram+ retain crystal violet (violet, thick peptidoglycan); Gram- decolorized by alcohol (pink, thin PG + LPS). [file:1]
Mycolic acids in cell wall resist decolorization with acid-alcohol after carbol fuchsin (ZN method). Red AFB vs blue non-AFB. [file:1]
Cocci (staph/strep), Bacilli (rods), Vibrio (comma, V.cholerae), Spirillum (rigid spiral), Spirochete (flexuous, Treponema). [file:1]

flashcards.front: Gram+ vs Gram- cell wall comparison?
flashcards.back: Gram+: Thick PG (15-80nm), teichoic acid, no OM. Gram-: Thin PG (2-7nm), LPS/OM/endotoxin, porins. [file:1]
India ink negative stain (clear halo), Quellung reaction (swelling with anticapsular Ab). Polysaccharide, anti-phagocytic. [file:1]
Monotrichous (single polar, V.cholerae), Amphitrichous (both poles), Lophotrichous (tuft one end), Peritrichous (all over). [file:1]
Adhesion to host cells (mannose-sensitive), sex pili for conjugation. Thinner/shorter than flagella. [file:1]
Endospores (Bacillus/Clostridium): heat/chemical resistant (dipicolinic acid), central/subterminal/terminal ± bulging. Stain modified ZN. [file:1]
E.coli: 20 min; M.tuberculosis: 20 hrs; M.leprae: 20 days. Binary fission. [file:1]
Metachromatic granules (volutin) in C.diphtheriae (polar bodies, bluish-purple). Chinese letter arrangement. [file:1]
Protoplast: Gram+ + lysozyme (no wall). Spheroplast: Gram- + lysozyme (partial wall remnants). Spherical. [file:1]
No membrane/nucleolus; single circular dsDNA (prokaryote); haploid; plasmids (R-factors/toxigenicity). [file:1]
Semipermeable (permeases), trilaminar lipoprotein, respiration site (mesosomes analogous to mitochondria). No sterols except Mycoplasma. [file:1]
Visualizes slender spirochetes/motility against dark background (oblique light). [file:1]
O-polysaccharide (serotype), Core PS, Lipid A (endotoxin: shock/pyrogen). [file:1]
Plasma membrane invaginations; respiratory enzymes, septum formation (mitochondria analog). More in Gram+. [file:1]
Gram+ cell wall (wall/lipoteichoic); antigens, ion binding. [file:1]
Cell wall defects (penicillin, autolysis); L-forms, protoplasts/spheroplasts. [file:1]
